source.title: Accident Investigation Report - MP 311 B Platform Pressure Release Incident

context.region: Gulf of Mexico, Main Pass Block 311

actions_taken: Production casing valves closed to isolate pressure
Injured personnel evacuated by Air-Med helicopter
Medical attention provided on-site and at University Medical Center
SCADA sensing lines removed and replaced with manual pressure monitoring
New pressure gauges and needle valves installed on all well casings
event.summary: On March 12, 2023, at 1633 hours, two Louisiana Safety Systems (LSS) technicians were injured on MP 311 B platform while working on well QAY B-18ST's production casing valves. A 1-inch stainless steel gas lift injection tubing rated at approximately 3100 psi ruptured due to 4330 psi of unexpected trapped pressure. One technician (IP #1) was knocked off a 4-foot aluminum stand and sustained serious injuries requiring helicopter evacuation. The second technician (IP #2) was knocked backwards by the force of escaping gas. The incident resulted from multiple failures: operators failed to monitor casing pressure, failed to perform required casing diagnostics, the facility was not built to approved drawings with tubing under-rated for actual pressures, and no pre-job hazard walkthrough was conducted. Workers were unaware of the 4330 psi pressure on the casing, believing it to be zero based on inaccurate SCADA readings from an isolated sensing line.
recommendations: Generate Safety Alert emphasizing the importance of accurate readings with regards to critical pressure data
Operators of dry trees on gas lift should ensure that their tubing and valves are rated per their approved drawings
Physically verify casing pressures rather than relying solely on SCADA systems
Conduct pre-job hazard walkthroughs to identify potential hazards
Perform casing diagnostics at required intervals
Ensure facility installations match approved drawings
key_phrases: 1 inch SS tubing rated 3100 psi ruptured
SCADA system inaccurate - sensing line isolated
facility not built to approved drawings
go figure it out, do your job

controls.name: Pressure rating of tubing and valves per approved drawings
controls.lod_basis: Physical design barrier to prevent equipment failure

supporting_text: the 1 inch SS production casing tubing line (rated for approximately 3100 psi) was overpressured by the 4330 psi flowing from the well
The gas lift tubing was actually rated for 3100 psi
The drawings show the gas lift check valve at the wellhead, the 2 inch casing valve, the 1 inch ball valve, and tubing are rated for 10,000 psi
The platform piping and tubing was not built to the approved drawings

controls.name: Casing pressure monitoring
controls.barrier_role: Detect pressure buildup before work activities

supporting_text: GOM Shelf failed to monitor the casing pressure
Operators were found to have not been monitoring casing pressures at the required intervals
the GOM Shelf operators had not been verifying casing pressures
Human Performance Error: Not aware of the hazards. Operators failed to monitor the casing pressure on the well

controls.name: SCADA system for casing pressure indication

supporting_text: they were relying on their SCADA system report which depended on a sensing line that was isolated from the casing pressure
A 3-day trending chart, obtained prior to the incident, printed out zero (0) pressures on the B-18 production casing
Because the casing valves were closed, this 0 psi reading on the SCADA system was inaccurate
All SCADA sensing lines that were used to monitor production casing pressures have been removed

controls.name: Casing diagnostic procedure
controls.barrier_role: Identify and remediate downhole integrity issues

supporting_text: GOM Shelf failed to carry out a vital casing pressure diagnostic
A casing diagnostic was not performed when the trigger pressure was reached
Human Performance Error: Not following procedures. Operators failed to perform a casing diagnostic as required by regulations
Had GOM Shelf performed this diagnostic, any downhole issues in the well could have been identified and resolved

controls.name: Pre-job hazard walkthrough and safety meeting

supporting_text: GOM Shelf failed to perform a pre-job safety meeting before starting work
The failure to perform a pre-job hazard walkthrough was confirmed in the GOM Shelf's internal investigative report
Operators did not perform a pre-job walkthrough to determine potential hazards
they were told by the PIC, go figure it out, do your job
their job started at 0600 hours with the signing of the LSS Job Safety Analysis (JSA) by the Person In Charge (PIC)
They were not advised of any special concerns or hazards that they needed to be aware of when asked
the two (2) LSS Technicians arrived, they were under the impression that there was zero (0) pressure on the B-18 well production casing

controls.name: Dual production casing valves
controls.lod_basis: Physical barrier to isolate pressure source

supporting_text: The B-18 well is an out-of-service well with dual 2 inch production casing valves installed flange to flange
IP #1 immediately attempted to shut the production casing valve closest to the tree
the approved SFDs for the facility only show one casing valve, but the well actually had 2 casing valves
A GOM Shelf Operator then closed the production casing valves on the B-18 well and isolated the pressure
the results were that the lower PTP was leaking
Two tubing plugs were set during this intervention and the tubing and casing were bled to 0PSI
The Production casing pressure on B-18 started increasing on 7/15/21
Leaking downhole plugs have been replaced

controls.name: Design and construction per approved drawings
controls.barrier_role: Ensure equipment is fit for purpose

supporting_text: The facility was not operating in accordance with approved drawings
BSEE reviewed the currently approved drawings for the facility and found multiple inconsistencies with the installation and the drawings
The platform piping and tubing was not built to the approved drawings. This directly led to the overpressure event

controls.name: Emergency Shutdown (ESD) system
controls.barrier_role: Isolate and shut down facility upon emergency

supporting_text: IP #2 headed up the stairs to get help and activated one of the Emergency Shutdown (ESD) stations along the way
Personnel on the top deck, after hearing the loud explosion, immediately activated the ESD as well
The platform was immediately shut in

controls.name: Emergency response and first aid
controls.barrier_role: Minimize injury severity through immediate medical response

supporting_text: IP #2 attempted to render aid to IP #1 who appeared dazed
IP #1 was then removed off the deck and carried to a safe location
IP #1 was placed on a stokes litter and carried to the top deck where he was brought into the Living Quarters
the Medic arrived to MP 311 B by boat, 2 hours after the incident occurred
The Medic provided medical attention to IP #1 to stabilize his injuries and then administered an IV

controls.name: Medical evacuation capability
controls.barrier_role: Transport injured personnel to advanced medical care

supporting_text: At 1830 hours, Air-Med (Acadiana) departed Lafayette after being contacted for emergency night helicopter service
At 2100 hours, the Air-Med arrived at MP 311 B
The Medical Technician arrived and prepared IP #1 for travel and then the Air-Med helicopter departed at 2135 hours for University Medical Center (UMC) in New Orleans

notes.rules: JSON output only. mentioned fields must be evidence-based. Use null for unknown values.
